objective: Add public WebSocket market-data streaming to the three exchanges that currently ship REST only — Bybit, OKX, and Bitget — in strict priority order (1) Bybit, (2) OKX, (3) Bitget, for a global (non-US) audience. Each exchange reaches full parity with the existing Binance and KuCoin streaming clients: ticker, trade, order-book (L2), and kline streams exposed through the same public IStreamClient surface via a new AddXxxStreams() DI extension (opt-in; REST-only consumers pay nothing). Clone the verified shared-streaming design exactly — do NOT introduce a new transport or engine. Reuse the shared StreamEngine + StreamServiceRegistration.AddStreams<TOptions>() body in CryptoExchanges.Net.Http; each exchange supplies only its variation points: a per-venue IStreamProtocol (subscribe/unsubscribe wire format, frame Classify, RoutingKeyFor, batch builders, ResolveConnectionAsync, HeartbeatPolicy, MinOutboundInterval) and a StreamDecoderRegistry of DeltaMapper decode closures (DTO->Core.Models), plus internal {Concept}Dto wire DTOs in the exchange's Dtos/Streaming/ folder. Respect each venue's real connection model and rate/heartbeat rules (Bybit v5 public spot topics; OKX public wss ping/pong + per-channel subscribe; Bitget public channels), setting MinOutboundInterval and batch caps per venue like Binance(200ms/100) and KuCoin(100ms). Honor K1 (Http never references Core.Models/DeltaMapper), K2 (subscribe-set replay), K3 (engine backoff), C1 (heartbeat execution in the engine). Spot-only, public streams only, delta callbacks only (no order-book maintenance/snapshotting). Tests per exchange mirror existing suites: protocol unit tests, decode tests, DI wiring tests, and a multi-symbol L2 order-book streaming smoke/integration test asserting at least one book update is delivered. Ship one PR per exchange to main (Bybit merged before OKX, etc.). Zero breaking change to the public IStreamClient surface.

objectives_history.objective: Add three new exchange integrations to CryptoExchanges.Net in strict priority order — (1) Bybit, (2) OKX, (3) Bitget — for a global (non-US) audience. Each exchange clones the verified Binance pattern (Core → Http → Exchange → DI, DeltaMapper DTO→model, bespoke ISymbolMapper, retry-only-on-GET, per-attempt re-signing). The signing/credential abstraction is generalized after Bybit, against OKX, and Bitget then reuses that abstraction.
Read-only MCP server (sub-project A of AI-agent-native roadmap)

Documentation & MCP-onboarding overhaul: a lean, polished README (exchange-icon status table, 60-second quick start, links out) + a public docs/ folder (getting-started, library usage with more examples, MCP server, per-client MCP setup for major clients, exchanges, architecture) + committed exchange SVG icons. No feature roadmap in public docs (opsec).

KuCoin exchange integration (5th exchange) — full parity: REST market data + account + trading with KC-API passphrase-v2 HMAC signing (per-attempt re-sign, retry-only-on-GET), bespoke ISymbolMapper (BTC-USDT), DeltaMapper DTO→model, AddKucoinExchange DI + MCP wiring, plus public WebSocket streaming (ticker/trade/order-book/kline) with auto-reconnect+resubscribe. Generalizes the streaming endpoint seam for KuCoin's token-negotiated bullet-public connection while keeping Binance's static-URL path unchanged. Spot-only, public streams only, no order-book maintenance.

Rename the all-exchanges DI aggregator package CryptoExchanges.Net.DependencyInjection → the root meta-package CryptoExchanges.Net (move AddCryptoExchanges + CryptoExchangesOptions to the CryptoExchanges.Net namespace; method/options shape unchanged), and decouple the five per-exchange unit-test projects from the aggregator (consolidate the all-exchanges resolution test into the renamed CryptoExchanges.Net.Tests.Unit). Clean swap — no consumers yet, no shim; version 0.5.0-preview.1, published set stays 9; no runtime behavior change. Refactor.

Fix the WebSocket public market-data streaming bug in CryptoExchanges.Net.Http.Streaming (StreamEngine): subscribing to N symbols emits an unpaced N-message control-frame burst that Binance rejects with PolicyViolation "Too many requests" (>5 msgs/sec), closing the socket before any data; reconnect replays the full subscribe-set as another burst, yielding an infinite reconnect loop with no order-book callback. Fix the shared StreamEngine so outbound control messages (subscribe/unsubscribe + reconnect replay) respect a per-venue message-rate limit (batching and/or pacing, configurable per exchange; Binance ~5/sec, KuCoin its own limit). Add a multi-symbol Binance+KuCoin L2 order-book regression test asserting at least one book update is delivered. Transport/wire/URL/keep-alive verified healthy — do not change those.
